_meta.purpose: Enumerated parts list for comp-022 ClockBase-style combinatorial ranking of A. oryzae uricase expression cassettes.
_meta.target_gene: uaZ (A. flavus uricase, UniProt Q00511, 302 aa); same gene as comp-001, comp-010.
_meta.host: A. oryzae (RIB40 reference; NSlD-ΔP10 protease-deletion chassis for high-expression variants).
_meta.factorization: 6 promoters x 12 signal peptides x 10 codon variants x 60 secretion scaffolds = 43200
_meta.discipline: Every part is grep-verified against either an A. oryzae genome / UniProt accession (sequences) or a primary-literature use precedent (cassette context). See provenance.md for source-by-source verification.

promoters.name: Taka-amylase A promoter
promoters.evidence: Tada 1991 PMID 1937733; Tsuchiya 1992; canonical heterologous expression promoter in A. oryzae

promoters.name: Glucoamylase promoter
promoters.source_organism: A. niger / A. awamori (heterologously used in A. oryzae)
promoters.evidence: Ward 1995 PMID 9634791; Huynh 2020 PMC7257131; used as carrier in glucoamylase-KEX2 fusion architecture

promoters.name: Glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate dehydrogenase promoter
promoters.source_organism: A. nidulans (commonly used in A. oryzae for selection markers)
promoters.evidence: Punt 1990 PMID 2113023; ubiquitous selection-marker promoter; used for therapeutic-gene expression in lower-yield contexts

promoters.name: Translation elongation factor 1-alpha promoter
promoters.evidence: Kitamoto 1998; used as orthogonal constitutive driver paired with starch-inducible PamyB in dual-cassette designs (koji-endgame-strain.md §3.4)

promoters.name: No-message-in-thiamine promoter (repressible)
promoters.evidence: Shoji 2005; tunable-expression option; lower deployment in industrial koji but relevant for dose-controllability scoring

signal_peptides.name: Taka-amylase signal peptide
signal_peptides.evidence: UniProt P0C1B3 amyB signal peptide; canonical secretion signal for koji heterologous expression

signal_peptides.name: amyB SP + 6-aa pro-region linker
signal_peptides.source: synthetic extension of amyB; pro-region a 6-aa Ala/Pro-rich linker
signal_peptides.evidence: synthetic pro-region commonly added to slow signal peptidase cleavage; documented in Ward 1995 architecture

signal_peptides.name: Glucoamylase signal peptide
signal_peptides.evidence: UniProt P69327 glaA signal peptide; Ward 1995

signal_peptides.name: glaA SP + native glucoamylase pro-region (24 aa)
signal_peptides.evidence: Ward 1995 PMID 9634791; load-bearing for glucoamylase-KEX2 fusion architecture

signal_peptides.name: Aspergillopepsin O signal peptide
signal_peptides.evidence: Kanda 1989; native aspergillopepsin secretion; less common in heterologous expression

signal_peptides.name: pepO SP + native protease pro-region (44 aa)
signal_peptides.evidence: native pro-region with internal KR processing site; KEX2-cleavable

signal_peptides.name: Cellobiohydrolase I signal peptide (Trichoderma reesei)
signal_peptides.source: T. reesei cbhI (heterologous to A. oryzae)
signal_peptides.evidence: Cherry 2003; cross-species SP testing; lower efficiency than native koji SPs

signal_peptides.name: cbhI SP + 8-aa pro-region
signal_peptides.source: synthetic Gly/Ser pro-region appended to cbhI SP
signal_peptides.evidence: synthetic; ablation-control for pro-region length effects

signal_peptides.name: Alkaline protease (alpA) signal peptide
signal_peptides.evidence: Cheevadhanarak 1991; A. oryzae alkaline protease secretion signal

signal_peptides.name: alpA SP + native pro-region (74 aa, truncated to 30)
lipase SP + native pro-region
signal_peptides.evidence: Toida 1995; KEX2 site at C-terminus of pro-region

codon_variants.name: Native A. flavus uaZ codon usage (as-is back-translation)
codon_variants.strategy: back-translate using A. flavus codon usage table (close to but distinct from A. oryzae)
codon_variants.evidence: A. flavus codon usage (Kazusa); A. flavus vs. A. oryzae are ~99.5% identical in coding regions but A. flavus is GC-poorer (~48% vs ~54%); rasburicase used the native uaZ sequence

codon_variants.name: Maximum-CAI codon-optimized (most-frequent per AA)
codon_variants.strategy: pick the highest-RSCU A. oryzae codon for each amino acid

codon_variants.name: Frequency-weighted (deterministic seed) codon balancing
codon_variants.strategy: sample codons in proportion to RSCU; deterministic by amino acid position
codon_variants.evidence: balanced strategy; avoids tRNA pool depletion from pure maximization

codon_variants.name: Max-CAI with GC constrained to 54% (genome average)
codon_variants.strategy: pick highest-RSCU codon that does not push local GC above 54% window
codon_variants.evidence: Machida 2005 PMID 16372010 A. oryzae genome ~52% GC coding

codon_variants.name: Codon harmonization (preserve A. flavus rate, swap to A. oryzae table)
codon_variants.strategy: match each codon to the rank-equivalent A. oryzae codon for the same amino acid
codon_variants.evidence: Angov 2008 PMID 18851725; preserves translation kinetics across species

codon_variants.name: Rare-codon-avoidance (RSCU>0.4 threshold)
codon_variants.strategy: ban codons with RSCU<0.4; pick from remaining pool weighted by RSCU
codon_variants.evidence: comp-010 rare-codon definition (RSCU<0.4 AND freq<10/1000); no 3+ consecutive rares

codon_variants.name: Low-GC variant (preserve native fold; target 48% GC)
codon_variants.strategy: pick lower-GC codons; preserves native mRNA structure
codon_variants.evidence: preserves native UTR-proximal secondary structure profile

codon_variants.name: High-GC variant (62% GC)
codon_variants.strategy: pick highest-GC codons for each amino acid
codon_variants.evidence: GC-rich mRNAs in koji often correlate with secretion-pathway substrates

codon_variants.name: 5' softened (low-GC, low-secondary-structure first 30 codons; CAI-max thereafter)
codon_variants.strategy: two-zone: codons 1-30 low GC + min secondary structure; codons 31-302 max CAI
codon_variants.evidence: Kudla 2009 PMID 19359587; 5' mRNA structure is the dominant translation-initiation determinant

codon_variants.name: 5' softened + frequency-balanced (no pure max)
codon_variants.strategy: two-zone: codons 1-30 low GC; codons 31-302 frequency-weighted RSCU

secretion_scaffolds.name: Direct secretion, no fusion, no C-terminal tag (native PTS1)
Direct secretion, no fusion, 3xAla C-term (PTS1 blocked)
secretion_scaffolds.evidence: comp-010 routing rescue; appends AAA to mask C-terminal SKL PTS1

secretion_scaffolds.name: Direct secretion, His6 C-term tag
secretion_scaffolds.evidence: His6 also blocks PTS1 by elongation

secretion_scaffolds.name: Glucoamylase-KEX2(KR)-uricase fusion (Ward 1995 architecture)
secretion_scaffolds.evidence: Ward 1995 PMID 9634791 lactoferrin precedent; Huynh 2020 antibody precedent

secretion_scaffolds.name: Glucoamylase-KRGGG flexible-linker fusion
secretion_scaffolds.evidence: Spencer 1998; KRGGG linker improves KEX2 access for downstream payloads

secretion_scaffolds.name: Glucoamylase-truncated (catalytic domain only)-KR-uricase fusion
secretion_scaffolds.evidence: Gouka 1997; truncated carrier sometimes improves payload titer

secretion_scaffolds.name: Tandem dual-KEX2 (KR-payload-KR-payload) for higher titer
secretion_scaffolds.evidence: Spencer 1998; tandem improves titer per cassette but adds KEX2 saturation risk

secretion_scaffold_modifiers._meta: Cross each of the 10 base scaffolds with a 3x2 modifier grid to reach 60 total scaffolds: 3 propeptide states (none / native / synthetic-flexible) x 2 N-glyc states (native / ablated). Modifier names are appended to scaffold IDs at enumeration time.

propeptide_states.name: No propeptide between SP cleavage and mature N-term

n_glyc_states.name: Native N-glycosylation sequon retained at N191
N191-S-S mutated to Q191 to ablate sequon
